title: luzula campestris var. calabra
intro: Luzula campestris var. calabra, also known as Calabrian Woodrush, is a variety of the Luzula campestris plant, a type of flowering plant in the family Juncaceae. The plant is traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever and soothing skin irritations. The leaves and roots of the plant can be used in herbal preparations, including infusions and tinctures, to take advantage of its medicinal properties. However, it is essential to note that there is limited scientific research on the plant's efficacy, and its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
